summary: Drew DeVault's November 2021 status update covers SourceHut's third anniversary as a public alpha, the decision to delay their new programming language (Hare) by up to a year due to slow progress on cryptography, the self-hosting compiler, and acceptance testing. He notes incremental progress including AES support, a FreeBSD port, and date/time API work. The visurf browser project saw contributions from new developers, and several smaller projects received minor updates.
key_points: SourceHut celebrated its third anniversary as a public alpha
The new programming language release was delayed by up to a year from the February 2022 target due to incomplete cryptography, self-hosting compiler, and acceptance testing
Visurf, a vi-style web browser, gained new contributors and features including configuration, URL filtering, and link following
Incremental language progress included AES cryptography support, FreeBSD porting, and date/time API design
money_quotes: Following a spooky month, we find ourselves again considering the progress of our eternal march towards FOSS world domination.
We have decided to delay the release of our new programming language, perhaps by as much as a year.
Things are looking good, but it will take longer than expected.
key_insight: Honest assessment of project timelines sometimes means accepting significant delays when key components like cryptography and self-hosting aren't ready, even when overall progress is positive.
